subject: Sociology
title: Academic Freedom and Power
description: A 60-second lesson on Academic Freedom and Power.

hook.text: A professor publishes controversial research. The university's donors complain. Who wins?

buildup.text: Academic freedom protects researchers' right to pursue truth without interference from politics or money.

discovery.text: Universities depend on funding from governments, corporations, and donors — all with agendas and interests.

twist.text: Adjunct professors, now the majority, lack tenure's protections. They self-censor to keep their fragile jobs.

climax.text: Academic freedom exists in principle but erodes in practice when money and politics shape what gets studied.

quiz.question: What threatens academic freedom in practice?
options: Dependence on donors, funding pressures, and lack of tenure
